app.title: Agentloom
app.tagline: Every conversation is a branchable DAG

sidebar.delete_confirm: Delete this conversation? This cannot be undone.
sidebar.delete_folder_confirm: Delete this folder and all conversations inside it? This cannot be undone.
sidebar.drop_to_root: Drop here to move to root

chatflow.empty: No chat nodes yet — send a message to start
chatflow.enter_workflow_hint: Click to open this node's workflow
chatflow.no_selection: Select a node to view the conversation
chatflow.delete_cascade: Delete this node and all descendants
chatflow.delete_cascade_confirm: This will delete this node and all its descendant nodes. Continue?
chatflow.fold_node_hint: This fold represents {{count}} hidden node(s). Right-click this node or its compact/pack host to unfold.
chatflow.fold_node_tokens_hint: Total context tokens across the folded range (entry_prompt + output_response) — approximately {{count}} tokens
chatflow.merge_badge_hint: This ChatNode merges two branches into one. Downstream turns see the merged summary; pre-merge history is unchanged.
chatflow.awaiting_user_hint: The agent has a question for you — drill in to see what it's asking
chatflow.compact_badge_hint: This ChatNode holds a summary of the chain above it. Downstream turns see the summary + recent turns instead of the full history.
chatflow.pack_badge_hint: This ChatNode packs {{count}} mid-chain ChatNode(s) into a single summary. From this node and its downstream, range members are hidden; from pre-pack siblings / global view they remain visible. Hover to highlight the packed range.
chatflow.chat_brief_fallback_hint: Produced by the deterministic code template — the LLM brief was skipped or failed.

workflow.empty: This node has no workflow steps
workflow.no_selection: Click a workflow node to inspect

conversation.input_placeholder_active: Send a message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
conversation.compact_truncated: {{count}} turn(s) above compacted — see summary below
conversation.view_effective_hint: Shows what the engine actually feeds the next LLM call — compact / pack / merge nodes appear as summaries. WYSIWYG.
conversation.view_original_hint: Shows the raw chat history (compact / pack / merge ops filtered out), reading like a typical chat agent.

compact_dialog.button_title: Summarize the chain up to this node into a new compact ChatNode
compact_dialog.title: Compact conversation
compact_dialog.hint: Walks the chain up to the currently selected node and produces a new compact ChatNode as its child. Downstream turns forked from the compact see the summary + preserved recent turns instead of the full history. Nothing is deleted — you can always pick an up-chain node to see the raw history.
compact_dialog.instruction_placeholder: e.g. Keep the final schema decisions and the reasoning behind them
compact_dialog.instruction_hint: Natural-language guidance the summarizer passes through to the compact worker.
compact_dialog.preserve_turns_hint: Overrides the ChatFlow's memory capacity for this one compact. Recent n messages stay verbatim; older ones get MemoryBoard references. Defaults to the ChatFlow setting.
compact_dialog.target_tokens_hint: Optional override of the summary's target size. Accepts raw tokens or k/M suffixes (32k = 32768, 1M = 1048576).

pack_dialog.title: Pack conversation range
pack_dialog.hint: Fold a contiguous ChatNode range (between two nodes related as ancestor-and-descendant on the primary-parent chain) into one pack ChatNode hanging off the range end. From the pack and its downstream the range is hidden; pre-pack / global canvas views still see it.
pack_dialog.use_detailed_index_hint: When on, each packed ChatNode becomes its own paragraph with a trailing id citation — downstream refs can point to individual members. When off, produce one monolithic summary.
pack_dialog.instruction_placeholder: e.g. keep the decision trail; drop process chatter
pack_dialog.preserve_last_n_hint: The last n ChatNodes in the range stay verbatim (not folded into summary). 0 = summarize everything (typical for a mid-chain pack).
pack_dialog.target_tokens_hint: Manual cap on the summary size. Accepts k/M suffixes.
pack_dialog.error_range_invalid: The two ChatNodes are not ancestor-descendant — they must be reachable along the primary-parent chain. Pick a different pair.

tools_panel.title: Tool visibility
tools_panel.description: Three-way control per tool. Default-allow tools are visible to every new chatflow automatically. Available tools are registered but hidden by default — each chatflow must opt in. Disabled tools are blocked everywhere in this workspace.
tools_panel.legend: Changes apply immediately to new LLM calls; in-flight requests already running are unaffected.

composer_model.title: Model for this turn
composer_model.hint: This model is used for this turn and is remembered until you change it again.

chatflow_settings.title: ChatFlow settings
chatflow_settings.default_model_hint: Used for every LLM call in this chatflow unless a node overrides it. Previously called default_model.
chatflow_settings.default_judge_model_hint: Routes pre/during/post judge calls to a separate model — useful for running a cheap verifier on top of an expensive main model. Inherit = same as draft model.
chatflow_settings.default_tool_call_model_hint: Model used for the draft that runs after tool results come back. Letting a small model handle tool-routing keeps the main turn cheap.
chatflow_settings.brief_model_hint: Model used by BRIEF WorkNodes and ChatBoard summaries — the per-node, per-turn, and per-WorkFlow briefs the engine writes into the MemoryBoard. Its context window must be at least as large as the draft model's. Inherit = same as draft model.
chatflow_settings.no_models_hint: No models configured yet. Add a provider and pin at least one model first.
chatflow_settings.execution_mode_auto_plan: Auto plan (with during judge, halts on budget)
chatflow_settings.execution_mode_native_react_hint: New turns go straight to the ReAct loop. No plan, no judges. Fastest, least controlled.
chatflow_settings.execution_mode_semi_auto_hint: Plan first, with pre/post judges to catch obvious issues. A failing judge bounces back to ask you.
chatflow_settings.execution_mode_auto_plan_hint: Adds the during judge on top of semi-auto. Halts and asks you when revise exceeds budget.
chatflow_settings.advanced_models_hint: Defaults used when the composer's model picker is left on "inherit". Composer picks are workspace-wide — override these only if this ChatFlow needs its own fallback.
chatflow_settings.judge_retry_budget_hint: How many times judge_post can ask the engine to redo failing subtasks before halting. Default 3. Use -1 for unlimited (the judge itself decides when to stop retrying).
chatflow_settings.runtime_environment_note_placeholder: Leave blank to use the workspace-language default template.
chatflow_settings.runtime_environment_note_hint: Prepended as a system message to every tool-bearing LLM call, alongside an auto-rendered OS / shell hint. Pushes back on "I don't have access"-style hallucinations from weaker models.
chatflow_settings.min_ground_ratio_hint: Halts a run whose planner churns without ever landing on a real tool_call. Expressed as a percentage of completed steps that must be tool calls. Default 5%. Leave empty to disable the fuse (useful for pure-reasoning tasks).
chatflow_settings.ground_ratio_grace_nodes_hint: Completed steps the fuse ignores before arming. Default 20 — protects short reasoning prefixes from tripping the fuse.
chatflow_settings.builtin_tools_hint: Untick to hide a tool from this chatflow's LLM calls. New chatflows inherit the workspace defaults (see Settings → Tools).
chatflow_settings.mcp_servers: MCP servers enabled in this chatflow
chatflow_settings.mcp_servers_hint: Untick a server to hide all of its tools from this chatflow's LLM calls.
chatflow_settings.mcp_no_global: No MCP servers are globally enabled. Add one in Settings → MCP first.
chatflow_settings.memoryboard_tags_hint: Each brief auto-extracts a set of concept anchors: produced are new concepts introduced this turn; consumed are upstream concepts referenced. memoryboard_lookup can retrieve by anchor in addition to free-text search. The two caps are hard ceilings the brief LLM is asked to obey — extras are truncated. Set to 0 to disable that side.
chatflow_settings.max_produced_tags_hint: Most concepts a brief may introduce per row. Default 10.
chatflow_settings.max_consumed_tags_hint: Most upstream concepts a brief may reference per row. Default 8.
chatflow_settings.compact_hint: Summarizes the oldest part of the message list when it grows too big for the target model's context window. Two independent tracks: the ChatFlow layer inserts a compact ChatNode before a new turn spawns; the WorkFlow layer inserts a compact WorkNode before each LLM call inside a running turn.
chatflow_settings.compact_chatflow_layer_hint: Evaluated at ChatNode spawn. Good for capping chain growth across many user turns.
chatflow_settings.compact_workflow_layer_hint: Evaluated before each LLM call inside a running turn. Good for capping growth inside a single turn (tool loops, multi-round judges).
chatflow_settings.chatnode_compact_trigger_pct: ChatFlow trigger threshold (% of context window)
chatflow_settings.chatnode_compact_trigger_pct_hint: When the prospective chain context for a new ChatNode crosses this fraction of the turn model's context window, a compact ChatNode is inserted first. Default 60%. Leave empty to disable this track.
chatflow_settings.chatnode_compact_target_pct: ChatFlow target size (% of context window)
chatflow_settings.chatnode_compact_target_pct_hint: Target footprint for the ChatNode-level summary. Default 40%.
chatflow_settings.compact_trigger_pct: WorkFlow trigger threshold (% of context window)
chatflow_settings.compact_trigger_pct_hint: When the pending message-list footprint crosses this fraction of the model's context window, a compact WorkNode is inserted before the LLM call. Default 70%. Leave empty to disable this track.
chatflow_settings.compact_target_pct: WorkFlow target size (% of context window)
chatflow_settings.compact_target_pct_hint: Target footprint for the summary — the compact worker is asked to bring the message list down to roughly this fraction of the context window. Smaller = more aggressive. Default 30%.
chatflow_settings.compact_target_tokens_hint: Two-way bound with the percent input. Context window = {{ctx}} tokens.
chatflow_settings.compact_preserve_mode_by_count_hint: Preserve the last n messages verbatim on compact, ignoring target volume entirely.
chatflow_settings.compact_preserve_mode_by_budget_hint: Budget-first: subtract the summary's tiktoken count from the target-volume budget, then greedy-pack the most recent raw messages from the tail until adding one more would overflow.
chatflow_settings.compact_keep_recent_count_hint: On compact, the n most recent messages are kept verbatim while older ones are replaced with MemoryBoard references. Smaller = more aggressive compaction. Ignored in target-volume mode, where tail size is driven by the volume budget. Default 3.
chatflow_settings.recalled_context_sticky_turns_hint: When a MemoryBoard entry is pulled back via get_node_context, it sticks to the context with a forget counter starting at n — decays by 1 each turn it isn't re-fetched, and re-compresses at 0. Larger = stronger recall. Default 3.
chatflow_settings.compact_model_hint: Model used by the compact worker that produces the summary. Inherit = use the chat's default model.
chatflow_settings.compact_require_confirmation_hint: When on, invoking compact from the UI opens a confirmation dialog (review instructions, must-keep / must-drop, dry-run). Off = compact immediately.

canvas_prefs.show_node_id_hint: Display the raw database node ID in the top-right corner of each node card. Helpful for debugging.
canvas_prefs.show_chatflow_id_hint: Display the ChatFlow's own ID after its title in the top header.
canvas_prefs.show_tokens_hint: Display total tokens (prompt + completion, cached separately) for each turn in the conversation panel.
canvas_prefs.show_gen_time_hint: Display wall-clock time from turn start to finish.
canvas_prefs.show_gen_speed_hint: Display output tokens per second (completion_tokens / seconds).
canvas_prefs.show_worknode_model_hint: Display the resolved model on every WorkNode card (draft, judge_call, tool_call). Off by default — only draft shows it.

providers.title: Providers
providers.delete_confirm: Delete this provider? This cannot be undone.
providers.name: Name
providers.api_key_none_hint: For local servers (Ollama, LM Studio) — no authentication header is sent.
providers.context_window_hint: Max context window tokens supported by this model
providers.json_mode_hint: How this provider accepts structured JSON output requests. "None" disables response_format; "Object" forces free-form JSON; "Schema" passes the full JSON Schema (Ollama, newer Ark, OpenAI gpt-4o+).
providers.json_mode_model_hint: Per-model override. Leave as "Inherit" to follow the provider default.
providers.sampling_toggle_hint: Toggle per-model sampling parameters. The available fields depend on the provider sub-type (e.g. top_k + num_ctx for Ollama, frequency_penalty for OpenAI). Empty = don't send; let the model's own default apply.
providers.sub_kind_hint: Picks the param whitelist used on every call. OpenAI 400s on unknown keys; Ollama needs num_ctx/top_k; Volcengine is OpenAI-compatible but needs thinking-enable; llama.cpp server accepts top_k + repetition_penalty, but num_ctx is fixed at server start (not per-request). Leave blank to send everything — not recommended for strict backends.
providers.sub_kind_required_for_sampling: Pick a Sub-type above before editing per-model parameters.
providers.local_provider_hint: For local servers (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, etc.): pick "Custom", set Type to "OpenAI Compatible", use their /v1 URL (e.g. http://localhost:11434/v1 for Ollama, http://localhost:1234/v1 for LM Studio), and choose "No key".

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.delete_confirm: Delete this MCP server? Its tools will disappear from active chatflows.
mcp.server_id_hint: Short identifier (lowercase, underscores) — appears in tool names as mcp__<id>__*.
mcp.enabled_hint: Disabled servers stay registered but don't connect or expose tools.
